message:
Unify CI build options under single CI_BUILD flag

Consolidate llms-txt generation and link checking into a single
CI_BUILD environment variable for simpler, composable CI builds.
Local builds are now faster by default, while CI gets full validation.

- Replace RUN_LINK_CHECK and separate llms-txt with CI_BUILD
- Rename build:linkcheck to build:ci in package.json
- Remove redundant nightly-linkcheck workflow
- Update docs.yaml and lint.yaml to use new CI_BUILD flag

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -55,7 +55,7 @@ jobs:
             pnpm astro build \
               --site "https://tenzir-docs-preview-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}.surge.sh"
           else
-            pnpm astro build \
+            CI_BUILD=true pnpm astro build \
               --site "${{ steps.pages.outputs.origin }}" \
               --base "${{ steps.pages.outputs.base_path }}"
           fi

@@ -100,4 +100,4 @@ jobs:
         run: pnpm sync:changelog
 
       - name: Run link check
-        run: pnpm run build:linkcheck
+        run: pnpm run build:ci

@@ -1,88 +0,0 @@
-name: Nightly Link Check
-
-on:
-  workflow_dispatch:
-  schedule:
-    # Run nightly at 02:00 UTC
-    - cron: "0 2 * * *"
-
-permissions:
-  contents: read
-
-jobs:
-  linkcheck:
-    name: Check Links
-    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
-    steps:
-      - name: Checkout repository
-        uses: actions/checkout@v4
-        with:
-          fetch-depth: 0
-          submodules: recursive
-          token: ${{ secrets.TENZIR_BOT_GITHUB_TOKEN }}
-
-      - name: Install pnpm
-        run: npm install -g pnpm
-
-      - name: Setup Node.js and pnpm
-        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
-        with:
-          node-version: "20"
-          cache: "pnpm"
-
-      - name: Install dependencies
-        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
-
-      - name: Run link check
-        env:
-          RUN_LINK_CHECK: true
-        run: pnpm build
-
-      - name: Create issue if links are broken
-        if: failure()
-        uses: actions/github-script@v7
-        with:
-          github-token: ${{ secrets.TENZIR_BOT_GITHUB_TOKEN }}
-          script: |
-            const title = `Broken links detected - ${new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]}`;
-            const body = `Automated link checking has detected broken links in the documentation.
-
-            **Workflow run:** ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
-
-            Please review and fix the broken links found during the build process.
-
-            This issue was automatically created by the link checking workflow.`;
-
-            // Check if there's already an open issue for broken links
-            const { data: issues } = await github.rest.issues.listForRepo({
-              owner: context.repo.owner,
-              repo: 'issues',
-              state: 'open',
-              labels: ['broken-links'],
-            });
-
-            if (issues.length === 0) {
-              await github.rest.issues.create({
-                owner: context.repo.owner,
-                repo: 'issues',
-                title: title,
-                body: body,
-                labels: ['broken-links'],
-              });
-            } else {
-              // Update existing issue with new run information
-              const existingIssue = issues[0];
-              const updatedBody = `${existingIssue.body}
-
-            ---
-
-            **New broken links detected:** ${new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]}
-            **Workflow run:** ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}`;
-
-              await github.rest.issues.update({
-                owner: context.repo.owner,
-                repo: 'issues',
-                issue_number: existingIssue.number,
-                body: updatedBody,
-              });
-            }

@@ -17,7 +17,7 @@
     "lint:eslint:fix": "eslint . --ext .js,.jsx,.ts,.tsx,.astro --fix",
